content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Fixed 3 milestone lineage issues: (1) Added round-trip tests in fs.rs that assert StartJournalDetails and CompletionJournalDetails stay in sync with their model.rs render counterparts — any field drift will cause test failure. (2) Changed update_task_run in fs.rs to accept a single open runless entry when a run_id is supplied, enabling backfill instead of rejecting with a CorruptRecord error; conflicting plan_hash values still block finalization. (3) Added plan_hash auto-population from the milestone snapshot in service.rs record_bead_start and update_task_run — when callers omit plan_hash, the snapshot's stored value (set by persist_plan) is forwarded to the lineage layer. Updated 1 existing test whose assertion changed from rejection to plan_hash conflict detection.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Issue 1: Added round-trip sync tests (start_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render and completion_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render) in src/adapters/fs.rs test module that render via model.rs, parse via fs.rs structs, and assert field-level equality plus render stability
2. [Completed] Issue 2: Changed the [index] if entries[*index].run_id.is_none() arm at fs.rs:3018 from returning CorruptRecord error to accepting the runless match, allowing run_id backfill during finalization. Existing plan_hash conflict and option_conflicts checks still guard against invalid transitions
3. [Completed] Issue 3: Added effective_plan_hash resolution in service.rs record_bead_start (line 428) and update_task_run (line 561) that reads snapshot.plan_hash as fallback when caller supplies None. Explicit caller-provided plan_hash takes precedence via map().or_else() chain
4. [Completed] Updated existing test completion_with_named_run_id_requires_exact_attempt_match → renamed to completion_with_conflicting_plan_hash_rejects_runless_match, asserting plan_hash conflict error instead of legacy runless rejection
5. [Completed] Added 3 new service-level tests: runless_start_completed_with_named_run_id (Issue 2 happy path), plan_hash_auto_populated_from_snapshot (Issue 3 with persist_plan → omitted plan_hash), explicit_plan_hash_takes_precedence_over_snapshot (Issue 3 precedence)
6. [Completed] Ran cargo test (683 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy -- -D warnings (clean), cargo fmt --check (modified files clean, pre-existing agent_execution_test.rs formatting unrelated)

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 683 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors on modified crate
- cargo fmt --check: service.rs and fs.rs pass; only pre-existing formatting diffs in unrelated tests/unit/agent_execution_test.rs
- start_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render: verifies all 3 fields (project_id, run_id, plan_hash) survive render→parse→render cycle
- completion_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render: verifies all 6 fields survive render→parse→render cycle
- runless_start_completed_with_named_run_id: starts with run_id=None, completes with run_id=Some('run-late'), verifies backfill and outcome
- plan_hash_auto_populated_from_snapshot: persists plan, starts bead with plan_hash=None, verifies snapshot plan_hash appears in lineage entry
- explicit_plan_hash_takes_precedence_over_snapshot: persists plan, starts bead with explicit hash, verifies caller value wins over snapshot
- completion_with_conflicting_plan_hash_rejects_runless_match: verifies plan_hash conflict still prevents invalid finalization after runless acceptance

## Outstanding Risks

- The round-trip tests detect field-level drift at test time, not compile time. A field addition that is only used in a code path not covered by tests could still silently drift until the round-trip test catches the serialization mismatch. A derive-macro or build-script approach would catch it at compile time but is heavier-weight.
- Pre-existing formatting drift in tests/unit/agent_execution_test.rs is unrelated to these changes but will appear in cargo fmt --check across the full workspace.
